pattern: The zero-valued nodes act as delimiters, so a single pass that resets an accumulator every time it crosses a zero and emits a new node just before resetting collapses each run into one merged value.
hints: Every zero marks the boundary between two groups of values to merge -- you never need to look more than one node ahead to know when a group ends.
Skip the very first node (it is always a leading zero), then walk the rest of the list keeping a running sum that resets to zero each time you hit a zero-valued node.

Each time you hit a zero-valued node (other than the very first), emit a new node holding the running sum so far, then keep going -- the last zero closes out the final group and there is nothing left to emit after it.

edge_inputs.description: two merged groups of different sizes
minimal 3-node list, a single one-element group
every group has exactly one element
duplicate group sums across different groups
values at the maximum node value in a single group
a long group followed by a short one

stress.description: many small random-length groups with random values, always starting and ending with the mandatory boundary zero
stress.note: no two zeros are ever adjacent because each group emits at least one non-zero value before its closing zero
